v1.letter_excerpt: The surgery is medically necessary to correct a severe jaw condition that is affecting the patient's quality of life and ability to eat properly. We ask that you reconsider the cosmetic classification...

v3.letter_excerpt: Cigna's own Medical Coverage Policy covers orthognathic surgery when there is an inability to chew that has caused documented weight loss. The patient has adopted a predominantly liquid diet as a direct result of the structural jaw condition — that is the functional impairment the policy requires. The initial submission omitted the dietary documentation; the supplemental records enclosed make the functional basis clear...

what_changed: Earlier drafts challenged the 'cosmetic' label generally without mapping the patient's clinical presentation to Cigna's specific functional impairment criteria.

The improved version quotes Cigna's criteria language, maps the liquid diet directly to the 'inability to chew' requirement, and correctly identifies the missing documentation as the root cause of the denial.
